title: ipconfig
summary: ipconfig is a Windows command-line tool used to view and troubleshoot IP configuration information.
facts: ipconfig shows IPv4 address, IPv6 address, subnet mask, and default gateway information.
ipconfig /all shows detailed adapter information, including DNS servers and MAC address.
ipconfig /renew requests a new DHCP lease.
ipconfig /flushdns clears the local DNS resolver cache.
examples: Use ipconfig /all to confirm which DNS server a Windows client is using.
Use ipconfig /release and ipconfig /renew when troubleshooting DHCP assignment issues.
commonMistakes: Using ipconfig to test connectivity. ipconfig shows configuration; ping tests reachability.
Forgetting that /flushdns clears cache but does not fix an incorrect DNS server setting.
examTips: For checking a client's assigned IP address, default gateway, or DNS server, think ipconfig.
scenarios: A Windows client has an APIPA address. Use ipconfig to confirm the address and then troubleshoot DHCP.
pbqIdeas: Given command output, identify whether the issue is DNS, DHCP, default gateway, or subnet related.
